content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[✓] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[✓] HELEN   [✓] OTHER Advice Files

FROM: Jeanne
DATE: 2/7/83

[✓] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 82013; 84211

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [X]

CALLER: Mattie Ropping, Forest Hill
REPRESENTING: Committee to Unify the School District
PHONE NUMBER: l=367-3189

QUESTION: Regarding school district unification proposal: unification can only occur by vote of the people. In order to start ballot measure qualification process, proposal must be approved by county board of education and then by state board of education. Costs incurred in connection with county and state board approval process are not reportable campaign expenditures.

ANSWER: However, if any studies, reports, etc. are used to attempt to influence the voters in connection with the qualification or election, those costs must be reported by committee.
